title: Persistent Save / Load — AsyncStorage Serialization
problem: All game state (XP, level, class, shared stash, graveyard, town portal scrolls) lives in Zustand in-memory only — it resets to zero every time the app is restarted. Players lose all progress on app close. This makes the game unshippable — no persistent progress means no retention.
analysis: All persistent state lives in gameStore (xp, level, classId, playerHp, playerMaxHp, graveyard, sharedStash, mana, maxMana, floor, tier, screen) and inventoryStore (bag, equipped). combatStore has townPortalScrolls which should survive app restarts. @react-native-async-storage/async-storage is included in the Expo SDK. The bag is run-scoped (reset on new run start in ClassSelectScreen) and should NOT be saved. Equipped items are cross-run and should be saved. The save/load cycle is: app launch → read AsyncStorage → hydrate stores → ready. State changes → debounced write to AsyncStorage.
implementation: 1. src/services/persistence.ts — SAVE_KEY='@d2game_save_v1', SETTINGS_KEY='@d2game_settings_v1'. SaveData interface: { version:1, xp, level, classId, playerHp, playerMaxHp, mana, maxMana, floor, tier, graveyard, classXp, sharedStash, equipped, equippedByClass?, townPortalScrolls, classesPlayed?, careerStats?: CareerStats (includes tiersCleared: number[] and ascensions: number), bossDefeated? }. SettingsData interface: { hapticsEnabled, audioEnabled, onboardingDone, hasSeenFirstBoss?, zoomLevel?: 0|1|2, hasSeenGhostEchoHint?: boolean }. 2. saveGame(data: SaveData): Promise<void> — JSON.stringify to AsyncStorage. 3. migrate(raw: any): SaveData | null — handles versionless saves, adds defaults for all optional fields including careerStats.tiersCleared and careerStats.ascensions. Returns null if version mismatch after migration. 4. loadGame(): Promise<LoadResult> — returns { save: SaveData, corrupted: false } | { save: null, corrupted: false } | { save: null, corrupted: true }. 5. clearSave(): removes SAVE_KEY from AsyncStorage (settings key survives). 6. saveSettings / loadSettings: separate AsyncStorage key so settings survive a save reset. 7. scheduleSave(): debounced 500ms module-level function — uses require() inside callback to break circular import between persistence ↔ stores. Reads gameStore, inventoryStore, gridStore snapshots; calls saveGame(). Called after any persistent state mutation (gainXp, endRun, depositToStash, withdrawFromStash, equipFromStash, unequipToStash, selectClass, ascendRun, etc.). 8. App.tsx hydration: loadGame() on mount → gameStore.hydrate() + inventoryStore.hydrateEquipped() + combatStore townPortalScrolls. Shows LoadingScreen while !isHydrated. 9. settingsStore.loadFromDisk(): called separately on mount, restores all SettingsData fields.

decisions.decision: Save only cross-run state — bag is NOT saved
decisions.rationale: The bag is run-scoped by design. Saving it would mean a crashed run persists its loot state, which undermines the roguelite death-resets-run contract. Only stash, equipped, xp, level, graveyard, and class survive.
alternativesConsidered: Save everything including bag — breaks roguelite death loop
Save nothing — already the broken current state

decisions.decision: Single save slot with version field, no multi-slot in v1
decisions.rationale: Multi-slot save requires a save slot selection UI before gameplay. For a solo mobile game in v1, one save slot is sufficient and keeps hydration path simple.
alternativesConsidered: 3 save slots — good for future, unnecessary complexity now

decisions.rationale: gainXp can be called multiple times per combat (level-up check). Writing to AsyncStorage on every XP tick would cause excessive I/O. 500ms debounce batches a full combat sequence into one write.
alternativesConsidered: Write on every state change — too frequent
Write only on app backgrounding — risk of loss on crash

successCriteria: Kill app mid-run, reopen — XP, level, class, stash items, graveyard entries, equipped items all restore correctly. Bag is empty on reopen (run-scoped). LoadingScreen shows briefly on cold start. clearSave() called from Settings wipes all data.
knownLimitations: No save migration across schema versions — version field exists, migrate() returns null on version mismatch (save wiped)
No iCloud/backup sync — save lives on device only
If app crashes mid-write, last 500ms of state changes may be lost
scheduleSave() uses require() inside callback to avoid circular module dependency — intentional, not a hot-path issue
componentFile: src/services/persistence.ts, src/store/gameStore.ts, src/store/inventoryStore.ts, src/store/combatStore.ts, App.tsx, src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial full feature definition for save/load persistence

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: BMAD session 2: update SaveData (careerStats now includes tiersCleared/ascensions), SettingsData (zoomLevel, hasSeenGhostEchoHint), scheduleSave pattern, migrate() defaults, LoadResult type

userGuide: Your progress saves automatically. XP, level, class, stash items, equipped gear, and graveyard entries survive app restarts. Your dungeon run (bag items, current floor) does NOT save — quitting mid-run is equivalent to dying. Start a new run from the menu after reopening.

publicInterface.name: loadGame
publicInterface.description: Reads, migrates, and validates save data from AsyncStorage. Returns LoadResult with corrupted flag.

publicInterface.name: clearSave
publicInterface.description: Removes main save key from AsyncStorage. Settings key is NOT removed.

publicInterface.name: scheduleSave
publicInterface.description: Debounced (500ms) save trigger. Call after any persistent state mutation. Uses require() inside timeout callback to avoid circular imports.

publicInterface.name: SaveData
publicInterface.description: Versioned save schema including careerStats.tiersCleared: number[] and careerStats.ascensions: number

publicInterface.name: SettingsData
publicInterface.description: Settings schema: hapticsEnabled, audioEnabled, onboardingDone, hasSeenFirstBoss, zoomLevel (0|1|2), hasSeenGhostEchoHint

codeSnippets.label: SaveData interface (current schema)
codeSnippets.snippet:
export interface SaveData {
  version:           1
  xp:                number
  level:             number
  classId:           ClassId | null
  playerHp:          number
  playerMaxHp:       number
  mana:              number
  maxMana:           number
  floor:             number
  tier:              number
  graveyard:         GraveyardEntry[]
  classXp:           Partial<Record<ClassId, { xp: number; level: number }>>
  sharedStash:       Item[]
  equipped:          Partial<Record<EquipSlot, Item>>
  equippedByClass?:  Partial<Record<ClassId, Partial<Record<EquipSlot, Item>>>>
  townPortalScrolls: number
  classesPlayed?:    ClassId[]
  careerStats?:      CareerStats  // includes tiersCleared: number[], ascensions: number
  bossDefeated?:     boolean
}

codeSnippets.label: SettingsData interface (current schema)
codeSnippets.snippet:
export interface SettingsData {
  hapticsEnabled:        boolean
  audioEnabled:          boolean
  onboardingDone:        boolean
  hasSeenFirstBoss?:     boolean
  zoomLevel?:            0 | 1 | 2
  hasSeenGhostEchoHint?: boolean
}

codeSnippets.label: migrate() careerStats defaults
codeSnippets.snippet:
careerStats: {
  totalRuns:    raw.careerStats?.totalRuns    ?? 0,
  deepestTier:  raw.careerStats?.deepestTier  ?? 1,
  totalKills:   raw.careerStats?.totalKills   ?? 0,
  bestQuality:  raw.careerStats?.bestQuality  ?? null,
  tiersCleared: raw.careerStats?.tiersCleared ?? [],
  ascensions:   raw.careerStats?.ascensions   ?? 0,
},

codeSnippets.label: scheduleSave — debounced with require() to break circular dep
App.tsx hydration on mount
